presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log serves as a test artifact documenting Q1 2024 vendor-control review progress, with control closure dependent on completing missing provenance and archiving tasks, while the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a conditional, pilot-level standing privilege for named administrators contingent on strict scoping, logging, quarterly reviews, and ongoing vendor revalidation. The former focuses on retrospective evidence completion for control closure, whereas the latter defines proactive, governed access parameters requiring continuous oversight and personnel change revalidation.

- Control closure hinges on completing missing provenance and archiving requirements for evidence items.  
- Access is granted as a pilot with strict scoping, logging, and quarterly review mandates.  
- Governance requires ongoing vendor confirmation and revalidation if personnel change.  
- The evidence log is a test artifact, not a production record.  
- Standing privilege is conditional on operational controls and recertification.  
- Material difference: retrospective evidence completion vs. proactive, ongoing access governance.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log serves as a test artifact documenting Q1 2024 vendor-control review progress, with control closure dependent on completing missing provenance and archiving tasks, while the Privileged Account Approval Email establishes a conditional, pilot-level standing privilege for named administrators contingent on strict scoping, logging, quarterly reviews, and ongoing vendor revalidation. The former focuses on retrospective evidence completion for control closure, whereas the latter defines proactive, governed access parameters requiring continuous oversight and personnel change revalidation.
Control closure hinges on completing missing provenance and archiving requirements for evidence items.
Access is granted as a pilot with strict scoping, logging, and quarterly review mandates.
Governance requires ongoing vendor confirmation and revalidation if personnel change.
The evidence log is a test artifact, not a production record.
Standing privilege is conditional on operational controls and recertification.
